oracle._comment: Gates src/triggers/oracle_alerts.py -- the CryptoScope Oracle quant signal (src/sources/cryptoscope_oracle.py), recomputed fresh every run from Kraken klines for every coin in watchlist.crypto. 'mode' switches the whole trigger between 'alert' (only post when a coin's read genuinely clears min_confidence -- see oracle_alerts.py's _meets_alert_bar) and 'rotation' (post every run regardless, cycling through every coin on a fixed schedule, with the same min_confidence bar only controlling the 🚨/⚠️ escalation prefix, not whether it posts at all) -- flip this one string to switch back, no code change needed. min_hours_between_alerts/max_alerts_per_run only apply in 'alert' mode. Deliberately NOT gated by ai_manager's day/night posting cap in either mode -- the throttle is a fixed UTC calendar-hour bucket instead (state.oracle_alerts.last_alert_hour_id), not an elapsed-minutes timer, specifically because a timer can skip an hour when the cron's exact firing second drifts -- see oracle_alerts.py's module docstring for the live-confirmed bug this replaced.

scheduled_daily._comment: feargreed_enabled=false (2026-07-19) drops the Fear & Greed Index post entirely -- market snapshot fires alone every day now. Set true to bring it back, with post_both controlling whether it posts alongside the snapshot every day (true) or rotates with it every other day (false) -- same as before.

filler._comment: Lowest priority of all -- only posts if nothing else fired this run. max_per_day bounds worst-case cost; with hourly checks, 24 would mean 'every empty hour' literally. Lower this (e.g. to 4-6) if filler ends up eating too much of the monthly budget -- see README cost math.

content_drafts._comment: Telegram-only draft ideas (never posted to X automatically) -- see src/triggers/content_drafts.py. Lower pct_change_trigger than price_alerts' since these are free (no X spend), just Telegram noise to weigh.

market_snapshot._comment: Telegram-only POC (2026-07-23) while X posting stays off -- see src/triggers/market_snapshot_telegram.py. Unconditional by design (not gated on a notable move, unlike price_alerts/content_drafts) so real output is visible every run to evaluate and tune the format, rather than possibly staying silent for hours. symbols defaults to watchlist.stocks (SPY/QQQ/AAPL) to stay fast/cheap on Twelve Data's free tier.
